title: How to Choose Project Management Software
description: A step-by-step guide to evaluating and selecting the perfect PM tool for your team.

sections.title: Define Your Requirements
sections.body: Start by mapping out your team's workflow and identifying pain points. List must-have features like task dependencies, Gantt charts, time tracking, or reporting. Prioritize these requirements to separate dealbreakers from nice-to-haves.

sections.title: Evaluate Your Budget
sections.body: Project management tools range from free tiers to enterprise plans costing hundreds per month. Factor in per-user pricing, setup fees, and the cost of any integrations you'll need. Choose a solution that fits your budget at current team size, but leaves room to grow.

sections.title: Test with Real Projects
sections.body: Sign up for free trials and run a real project through each candidate tool. Involve your team early to collect genuine feedback on usability and performance. A tool that looks good in a demo may fall short under real workloads.

sections.title: Check Integrations
sections.body: The PM tool you choose should connect seamlessly with your existing stack — Slack, email, calendars, file storage, and your CRM. Review each platform's integration marketplace and API documentation. Poor integration support can create silos that defeat the purpose of a central tool.

sections.title: Consider Scalability
sections.body: A tool that works for a five-person startup may buckle under a fifty-person org. Evaluate how each platform handles permission structures, cross-project reporting, and automation at scale. Choose one that can grow with your company without forcing a painful migration later.

author: StackPilot Team
